metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add S5 as an explicit dependency in the Dependencies section, alongside S1, since the loop requires S5's service-account credentials, SSM secret, and permission grants (Submit + `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader`) to make any Gerrit API call.
findings.finding: S5 is referenced throughout the plan as providing authentication credentials and permission grants that S2 depends on for every API call and submit, but S5 is absent from the Dependencies section — only S1 is listed. This is a cross-section contradiction: the Preconditions section declares S5 provisioning as 'required live for this loop,' but the Dependencies section (which governs sequencing and blockers) omits S5 entirely, so an implementer following the Dependencies section alone would start S2 without S5 being live and every `POST /submit` and rebase call would fail with 403.
findings.location: Dependencies section vs. Preconditions section and 'What' section (S5 AC2 references)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Dependencies section: 'Also depends on **S5** — the bot service account, its SSM-backed HTTP password, and its ACL grants (Submit on `refs/heads/*`, `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` capability) must be provisioned before S2's loop can authenticate or submit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `pygerrit2` rationale with the actual prior-art client: the autolander should reuse or extend `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` (stdlib urllib, already auth-wired and in production) rather than introducing `pygerrit2` as a new third-party dependency that does not exist in the repo.
findings.finding: The plan designates `pygerrit2` as the 'shared client (prior art)' for Gerrit REST access, but the actual prior-art Gerrit client in the codebase is `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py`, which is deliberately STDLIB-ONLY (urllib/subprocess) with an explicit comment '— deliberately NO new httpx dependency'. `pygerrit2` appears nowhere in pyproject.toml, infra/, or src/. Adopting pygerrit2 would introduce a new third-party dependency that directly contradicts the established project pattern, add a new supply-chain surface, and diverge from the client already exercised in production — the opposite of the stated rationale.
findings.location: What section — 'Gerrit REST access uses the shared pygerrit2 client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client, don't reimplement)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the What section to: 'Gerrit REST access reuses/extends the existing `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` client (stdlib urllib — the established project pattern); new autolander-specific endpoints (rebase, submit, related-changes) are added as methods on that client or a thin subclass in infra/autolander/.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the chain-rebase endpoint notation throughout the plan: replace `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` with `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` + body `{"rebase_chain": true}` (the actual Gerrit 3.x REST interface), and verify this field name against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance in the E2E probe.
findings.finding: The plan uses `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` as the endpoint for chain rebase throughout the AC and What sections. In the Gerrit 3.x REST API, chain rebase is performed via `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with a `RebaseInput` body field `rebase_chain: true` — there is no `:chain` path suffix. If implemented literally as written, every chain-rebase call will receive a 404, silently failing to rebase the stack and landing only the tip.
findings.location: AC — 'Rebase-selection rule': 'rebases with POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain'; What section: same notation
findings.suggested_fix: In AC rebase-selection rule: 'a chain of >1 non-merge member rebases with `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` body `{"rebase_chain": true, "rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader": true}`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'rebase:chain' notation before implementation: confirm (via the Gerrit 3.14.1 REST docs or a direct probe of the live instance) whether chain rebase is `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with body `{"rebase_chain": true}` or another form, and update AC2 and the E2E test AC to use the verified endpoint path.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 uses 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain' notation for chain rebase, but this is not a valid Gerrit REST API path. In Gerrit 3.x (including 3.14.1), chain rebase is performed via POST /changes/{id}/rebase with a RebaseInput body containing 'rebase_chain: true' — there is no ':chain' URL suffix form. The plan itself flags this as 'Precondition to probe against the live instance: the rebase:chain path' but leaves it unresolved, and the test AC requires proving 'the rebase:chain path' works in the live E2E check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 'Rebase-selection rule': 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain' and Verification/tests AC: 'proving the rebase:chain path'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain' with 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase with body {"rebase_chain": true, "rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader": true}' (the Gerrit 3.5+ RebaseInput chain field) and remove the 'Precondition to probe' hedge once confirmed against the live instance.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the exact chain-rebase REST surface against live Gerrit 3.14.1 docs or the running instance (check whether it is `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `{rebase_chain: true}` in the body, or a distinct `:chain` path) and update AC2's endpoint reference to the confirmed form.
findings.finding: The plan uses `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` as the chain-rebase endpoint path, but the Gerrit REST API uses `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `rebase_chain=true` in the `RebaseInput` body — the colon-suffix path form (`rebase:chain`) is not a documented Gerrit REST endpoint path and is likely a notation error that will produce 404s at runtime.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'detect a relation chain via `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related`… a chain of >1 non-merge member rebases with `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` with `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with body `{"rebase_chain": true, "rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader": true}` if that is confirmed to be the correct Gerrit 3.14.1 form — then gate this on the SPIKE probe outcome.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `pygerrit2` dependency claim with an explicit directive to extend the existing `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.GerritClient` (or a thin sibling in `infra/autolander/`) with the new Gerrit REST verbs the lander needs (rebase, related-changes, submit, label-removal), consistent with the codebase's established stdlib-only HTTP client policy.
findings.finding: The plan introduces `pygerrit2` as a new third-party dependency framed as 'prior art: reuse the de-facto client', but pygerrit2 appears nowhere in the codebase (absent from pyproject.toml, not in any import, not resolvable in the environment). The repo already has a production-grade, intentionally stdlib-only `GerritClient` at `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` with 4+ active call-sites (voter.py, app.py, reconcile.py, dedup.py). The existing client was deliberately written with 'STDLIB ONLY (urllib/subprocess) — deliberately NO new httpx dependency' and already handles auth, XSSI stripping, and all Gerrit REST verbs the lander needs. Adding pygerrit2 would introduce a new third-party dep, diverge from the codebase's established HTTP client policy, and NIH-bypass the existing production implementation — the inverse of what the plan claims.
findings.location: What section: 'Gerrit REST access uses the shared **pygerrit2** client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client, don't reimplement)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, replace 'Gerrit REST access uses the shared **pygerrit2** client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client, don't reimplement)' with 'Gerrit REST access wraps the existing `GerritClient` (or a thin `infra/autolander/` sibling using the same stdlib `urllib`/Basic-auth pattern) — no new third-party HTTP dependency; add only the missing verbs (POST /rebase, GET /related, POST /submit, DELETE label).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `pygerrit2` reference with the actual prior art: `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` (stdlib-only `GerritClient`); either specify that `infra/autolander/loop.py` extends/reuses this client or document why a new dependency is warranted and add it to the project's dependency list.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Gerrit REST access uses the shared **pygerrit2** client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client, don't reimplement)', but the actual prior art in the codebase is a bespoke stdlib-only (`urllib`/`subprocess`) `GerritClient` at `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` — which explicitly documents 'STDLIB ONLY (urllib/subprocess) — deliberately NO new httpx dependency'. `pygerrit2` is not referenced anywhere in the repo and is UNRESOLVED in the installed environment.
findings.location: What section: 'Gerrit REST access uses the shared **pygerrit2** client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client, don't reimplement)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'Gerrit REST access uses the shared **pygerrit2** client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client, don't reimplement)' to 'Gerrit REST access reuses the existing bespoke `GerritClient` from `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` (stdlib `urllib`-only; extend it with rebase/submit endpoints rather than adding a new dependency).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `pyproject.toml` (or the equivalent infra requirements file) to the plan's file-impact list as an edit target to declare `pygerrit2` as a project dependency.
findings.finding: `pygerrit2` is named as the Gerrit REST client the service will use, but it appears nowhere in the repo's dependency manifests (`pyproject.toml`, any requirements file, or infra scripts) and is unresolvable in the installed environment. The plan names no dependency file as an edit target to add it.
findings.location: What section: '…the shared **`pygerrit2`** client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client, don't reimplement), wrapped thinly'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `file_impact` entry for `pyproject.toml` (or `infra/autolander/requirements.txt` if the service has its own manifest) noting 'add pygerrit2 dependency', and include a `set_verify_commands` DD entry confirming it is importable in the service's runtime environment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the pygerrit2 claim: the repo's established pattern is a thin stdlib `urllib`-based client (see `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py`); either explicitly adopt that pattern for `infra/autolander/loop.py` (referencing the actual prior art), or justify introducing pygerrit2 as a new dependency with a concrete capability gap that the stdlib client cannot fill.
findings.finding: The plan claims 'Gerrit REST access uses the shared pygerrit2 client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client, don't reimplement)', but pygerrit2 is not present anywhere in this codebase: it appears in neither pyproject.toml, nor any requirements file, nor any import in src/ or infra/. The existing Gerrit REST client (src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py) is explicitly stdlib-only by deliberate design ('STDLIB ONLY (urllib/subprocess) — deliberately NO new httpx dependency'). Describing pygerrit2 as 'the shared client' misrepresents the codebase's actual prior art and proposes introducing a new unvetted third-party dependency under the guise of reuse.
findings.location: What section — 'Gerrit REST access uses the shared pygerrit2 client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client, don't reimplement)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the shared pygerrit2 client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client)' with 'the same thin stdlib urllib pattern as src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py (the established in-repo Gerrit REST client — no new third-party HTTP dependency)'. If pygerrit2 is genuinely needed for a specific feature (e.g., event streaming), state the gap explicitly and add it to pyproject.toml with a rationale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `pygerrit2` 'prior art' claim with a direct reference to the existing `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` bespoke client as the actual prior art, and either extend it or create a parallel `infra/autolander/gerrit_client.py` using the same stdlib-only pattern — drop `pygerrit2` from the design.
findings.finding: `pygerrit2` is described as 'prior art: reuse the de-facto client' but is absent from `pyproject.toml` and the installed environment; the actual prior art in this codebase is a bespoke stdlib-only `GerritClient` in `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py`. Introducing pygerrit2 is a NET-NEW unverified dependency, not a reuse.
findings.location: What section — 'Gerrit REST access uses the shared **pygerrit2** client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client, don't reimplement)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, replace 'Gerrit REST access uses the shared **pygerrit2** client (prior art: reuse the de-facto client, don't reimplement)' with 'Gerrit REST access uses the same stdlib-only HTTP Basic Auth pattern as `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` (the existing project prior art); a thin `infra/autolander/gerrit_client.py` extends that client with the rebase, related-changes, and submit endpoints.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace each 'Precondition to verify/probe' parenthetical with a stated default: e.g. 'if ApprovalInfo.date is absent, fall back to change number as FIFO key' and 'if rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader is rejected with 4xx, fail the rebase step and hand back via S3 mechanism'.
findings.finding: Two inline parenthetical notes ('Precondition to verify against the live Gerrit 3.14.1: that ApprovalInfo.date is populated' and 'Precondition to probe against the live instance: the rebase:chain path and the rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader body key') are 'verify whether' / 'check if' placeholders that defer a real design decision to the executor: if the probe result is negative, no fallback is stated. Specifically, if ApprovalInfo.date is absent for the Autosubmit label, FIFO ordering has no defined fallback key; if rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader behaves unexpectedly, there is no stated error path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 (FIFO ordering parenthetical) and AC2 (rebase-selection parenthetical)
findings.suggested_fix: For AC1 add: 'Fallback if ApprovalInfo.date is absent for this label: order by change._number ascending (proxy for submission order).' For AC2 add: 'If rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader is rejected by the server, fail the rebasing phase and hand back via S3's needs_rebase mechanism rather than retrying without the field.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'S3's needs_rebase marker' (type, location, and schema) in either this plan or an explicit cross-reference to S3's spec, so AC4 and AC8 tests can assert its correct production.
findings.finding: AC4 references 'S3's needs_rebase marker' as something S2 must write during N=5 hand-back, but nowhere in the plan is this artifact defined — its type (a file on disk, a Gerrit label value, a database row, an in-memory flag?) is never stated. The implementer cannot verify they have correctly written the marker, and AC8's tests cannot confirm correctness without knowing what to assert.
findings.location: AC4 (TOCTOU hand-back description): 'writes S3's needs_rebase marker'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the ApprovalInfo.date precondition: probe the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance for a change carrying the Autosubmit label and confirm the field is populated; add a documented fallback ordering key (e.g., change number ascending) for when the field is absent or null.
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 FIFO ordering contract depends on 'ApprovalInfo.date' being populated for the custom Autosubmit label (via the DETAILED_LABELS query option), but the plan itself flags this as an unresolved precondition: '(Precondition to verify against the live Gerrit 3.14.1: that ApprovalInfo.date is populated for the custom Autosubmit label; existing repo code reads only the integer value.)'. No fallback ordering key is specified if this field is absent. The FIFO guarantee is a core correctness property of the lander, not an implementation detail.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'FIFO ordering keyed on the Autosubmit vote's timestamp — read from the ApprovalInfo.date field' and the parenthetical precondition

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Convert the two inline '*(Precondition to verify…)*' notes into a mandatory pre-implementation SPIKE AC (e.g., AC0) that probes the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance for `ApprovalInfo.date` population on a custom label and the exact `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` key + chain-rebase endpoint surface — gate the main implementation story on SPIKE completion.
findings.finding: The two inline '*(Precondition to verify against the live Gerrit 3.14.1: …)*' notes in AC1 and AC2 explicitly flag `ApprovalInfo.date` population for a custom label and the `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` body key + `rebase:chain` path as UNVERIFIED capabilities — yet the plan commits the full implementation story without routing these to a SPIKE first. Both are load-bearing: FIFO ordering fails silently if `date` is null, and every rebase call fails if the body key name is wrong.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 parenthetical '*(Precondition to verify against the live Gerrit 3.14.1: that `ApprovalInfo.date` is populated for the custom `Autosubmit` label…)*' and AC2 parenthetical '*(Precondition to probe against the live instance: the `rebase:chain` path and the `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` body key.)*'

findings.finding: The Scope section explicitly excludes failure paths ('Failure paths, deploy, and the agent-facing command are separate stories'), yet AC4 mandates a detailed failure/retry path — a bounded re-drive loop (N=5), a parking action, and surfacing 'needs_rebase' — all of which are failure-path behavior. This creates a cross-section contradiction: an implementer reading Scope would defer these behaviors to a future story, while the ACs require them here.
findings.location: Scope section vs AC4 ('re-drive the same `wipChain`... looping at most N=5 times; after N=5 re-drives park the change and surface `needs_rebase`')
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to Scope: 'Exception: the TOCTOU re-drive exit condition (N=5 cap → park → `needs_rebase`) is in-scope here as it is integral to the safe-submit invariant.' This disambiguates without expanding scope unexpectedly.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` and `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` as edit targets (or confirm S5's file-impact list covers them), and add S5 to this plan's Dependencies section so credential provisioning is sequenced correctly.
findings.finding: The plan's autolander credential path references 'S5 AC2' as the source of the SSM-backed HTTP password for `RebarBotNava`, but the existing SSM parameter manifest (`infra/terraform/ssm.tf`) has no slot for this credential, and `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` does not map it. Both files are existing consumers that would need a new entry — yet neither is enumerated in the plan's scope or file-impact list.
findings.location: What section: 'the bot authenticates as the `RebarBotNava` service user via its SSM-backed HTTP password (S5 AC2)'; Preconditions section; S5 not listed in Dependencies

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify and validate the ancestor-atomic submit mechanism: confirm whether Gerrit 3.14.1 co-submits relation-chain ancestors when the tip is submitted (ancestry-based), or whether atomicity requires all members to share a Gerrit topic (topic-based via submitWholeTopic), and make the AC prescribe the correct one — including whether the autolander must assign a shared topic to chain members.
findings.finding: The ancestor-atomic submit mechanism conflates two distinct Gerrit atomicity mechanisms: (1) relation-chain co-submission (Gerrit submits all ancestors of a change when the chain tip is submitted, server-side) and (2) `change.submitWholeTopic = true` (topic-based atomic co-submission). ADR-0040 attributes atomic stack landing to `submitWholeTopic` (topic-based). Gerrit's native relation-chain co-submission is a separate behaviour that depends on whether Gerrit is configured to submit relation chains atomically — and under FFO the exact behaviour of a single `POST /changes/{tip}/submit` propagating to all ancestors is not confirmed in any project doc. If chain members don't share a topic (they share only a Git ancestry), `submitWholeTopic` does not help, and the relation-chain ancestor-atomicity depends on Gerrit's built-in relation-chain submit semantics, which the plan cites ('relation-chain semantics') but does not validate against the live 3.14.1 config.
findings.location: AC — 'Ancestor-atomic submit' paragraph: 'a single POST /changes/{tip-id}/submit on the chain tip; under FFO with change.submitWholeTopic/relation-chain semantics this submits the tip together with all its ancestors atomically'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm via the SPIKE (or an existing Gerrit 3.14.1 test) whether `POST /changes/{tip-id}/submit` atomically submits all relation-chain ancestors without topic tagging under FFO, or whether `submitWholeTopic` requires a shared topic — and update AC4 with the confirmed submit mechanism.
findings.finding: The ancestor-atomic submit path asserts that `POST /changes/{tip-id}/submit` submits all chain ancestors atomically under relation-chain semantics, but `submitWholeTopic` (confirmed live in `gerrit.config`) operates on *topics*, not relation chains. ADR-0040 says 'a topic's linear changes submit atomically' — relation chain submit atomicity is a distinct mechanism and its behavior under FFO + `submitWholeTopic` for relation chains (not topic-tagged changes) has not been confirmed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'Ancestor-atomic submit = a single `POST /changes/{tip-id}/submit` on the chain tip; under FFO with `change.submitWholeTopic`/relation-chain semantics this submits the tip together with all its ancestors atomically'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Scope/AC4 whether S2 *calls a shared function defined in S3* (making S3 a code dependency) or *independently implements* the same label-removal + comment actions — and if the former, add S3 to the Dependencies section.
findings.finding: The Scope section says 'The N=5-exhaustion park reuses S3's needs_rebase hand-back mechanism' — implying S3 owns and implements the hand-back code. But AC4 then describes S2 itself performing the full hand-back inline: the bot 'removes the Autosubmit label from every member of the stack, posts a Gerrit comment… and writes S3's needs_rebase marker.' This contradiction leaves the implementer unclear about whether S2 calls into S3's shared code/function, or whether S2 re-implements label removal and commenting independently.
findings.location: Scope section ('N=5-exhaustion park reuses S3's needs_rebase hand-back mechanism') vs. AC4 TOCTOU guard subsection

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a live/E2E verification step for the TOCTOU re-drive path (e.g. a scripted race that advances main between rebase and submit, confirming the bot re-drives rather than submitting stale), OR explicitly document why this path is unit-test-only and which mock fidelity guarantee makes that sufficient.
findings.finding: The live/E2E check covers only the happy path (select→rebase→await-Verified→submit). The TOCTOU guard re-drive and the N=5 exhaustion hand-back — the plan's primary safety properties — are verified by unit tests only, with no rationale given for the absence of a live exercise. Under E6(f), the re-confirmation step and the `not fast-forward` refusal branch are new production behaviors that only execute against a live Gerrit instance; green offline unit tests bypass the risky path (the live FFO refusal).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 (TOCTOU guard + N=5 re-drive) and the Verification/tests AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit unit-test bullet for the no-vote-cast guarantee (e.g. 'assert no call to POST /changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/review is ever made by the loop') and name the concrete proving command (e.g. grep or a spy-assertion) rather than deferring all commands to DD entries.
findings.finding: The no-vote-cast guarantee (AC: 'Reads existing Verified votes only; casts NO votes') has no explicitly stated proving command or named test in the Verification/tests AC. The unit test coverage bullet list names five specific behaviors but omits this criterion, leaving it without a mapped verification step.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC7 ('Reads existing Verified votes only; casts NO votes') and the Verification/tests AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Verification/tests AC: 'A spy/mock assertion confirms that no POST .*/review endpoint is called during any loop phase; proven by: grep -rn "POST.*review" infra/autolander/ returning zero results outside the test fixtures.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit test step in AC8 (or AC1) that probes the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance to confirm `ApprovalInfo.date` is non-null for the `Autosubmit` label — and specifies the fallback behavior if it is null.
findings.finding: AC1 calls out a specific precondition to verify ('that `ApprovalInfo.date` is populated for the custom `Autosubmit` label'), but AC8 (the verification/tests AC) does not mention verifying this precondition. AC8's live/E2E check is described as proving the `rebase:chain` path and `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` key — the `ApprovalInfo.date` population check is an orphaned precondition with no corresponding test coverage.
findings.location: AC1 (precondition note) vs. AC8 (Verification/tests)
findings.suggested_fix: In AC8, add: 'A pre-flight probe confirms `ApprovalInfo.date` is populated for a test `Autosubmit` vote on the live instance; if null, the FIFO selector must document its fallback ordering and a follow-up story is filed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit pre-build probe task: issue a `GET /changes/?q=label:Autosubmit+1&o=DETAILED_LABELS` against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance, confirm `ApprovalInfo.date` is non-null for the custom `Autosubmit` label, and record the result as a `set_verify_commands` DD entry before implementing the FIFO selection logic.
findings.finding: `ApprovalInfo.date` being populated for the custom `Autosubmit` label on Gerrit 3.14.1 is flagged as a precondition to verify, but no explicit pre-build probe or spike step is planned — the E2E test covers rebase paths but not this specific field. If unpopulated, FIFO ordering silently degrades (no error, wrong ordering).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first AC bullet: '*(Precondition to verify against the live Gerrit 3.14.1: that `ApprovalInfo.date` is populated for the custom `Autosubmit` label…)*'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the N=5 TOCTOU re-drive limit configurable (e.g., via an environment variable or config file with default 5), consistent with how the 30-min Verified timeout is already described as 'default… configurable'.
findings.finding: The N=5 TOCTOU re-drive limit (`re_drive_count`) is a tunable business-rule threshold described in the `wipChain` schema but is not stated as configurable — it appears to be a hardcoded constant.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `wipChain` schema AC and TOCTOU guard AC: '…looping **at most `N=5` times** (`re_drive_count` in the schema below)…'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `MAX_REDRIVES: int = int(os.environ.get('AUTOLANDER_MAX_REDRIVES', 5))` to the loop's config and reference it in the `wipChain` logic; document the env var in the ADR.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit retry/backoff policy (e.g. exponential backoff with jitter, max 3 retries) for all Gerrit REST calls, distinguish retryable (5xx, timeout) from non-retryable (400, 403, 409) errors, and define the bot's state-machine behavior when retries are exhausted (log + transition to `idle` or `paused`).
findings.finding: No retry, backoff, or graceful-degradation strategy is specified for transient Gerrit REST API failures (5xx, network timeout, connection refused) at any point in the loop — query, rebase, Verified poll, or submit. A single unhandled exception on a REST call would either crash the loop or silently abandon the in-flight wipChain, leaving a rebased-but-unsubmitted change stranded mid-flight.
findings.location: What section / Acceptance Criteria — no AC addresses REST error handling
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the pygerrit2 client in a thin retry decorator (e.g. `tenacity.retry` with `wait_exponential`, `stop_after_attempt(3)`, retrying on `requests.exceptions.ConnectionError` / HTTP 5xx). On retry exhaustion: log the error, clear `wipChain` to `idle` (safe because FFO prevents double-submit), and emit a metric/alert. Document this in an AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either persist `re_drive_count` (e.g. as a Gerrit hashtag or comment on the change, or a lightweight file-backed counter) so it survives process restarts, or document and accept the restart-resets-counter behavior with a rationale (e.g. restarts are rare and the guard is best-effort).
findings.finding: `re_drive_count` is stored only in the in-memory `wipChain` record. A process restart (crash, OOM kill, deploy) resets this counter to zero, silently bypassing the N=5 TOCTOU exhaustion guard. A change that has already driven the lander into 4 re-drives will restart its counter after a crash, allowing an unbounded number of total re-drives across restarts.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `wipChain` schema AC; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Encode the re-drive count in a Gerrit hashtag (e.g. `autolander-redrives-N`) written by the bot on each re-drive; read it on startup when re-selecting a wipChain. This is a single REST call and keeps state durable without requiring a database.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC specifying that each distinct failure outcome (rebase conflict, submit rejection, CI timeout, N=5 exhaustion hand-back, Gerrit REST error) emits a structured log entry and/or a named counter metric, and that the N=5 exhaustion triggers an operator-visible alert.
findings.finding: Observability beyond the liveness heartbeat is unspecified. Specific failure points — rebase failures, submit rejections (non-TOCTOU), CI timeouts, and N=5 exhaustion hand-backs — have no stated metric, structured log entry, or alert. Operators cannot distinguish 'bot is healthy but waiting for CI' from 'bot is stuck because Gerrit is rejecting rebases'.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Verification/tests AC; What section (heartbeat mention)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a startup-reconciliation step: on boot, query Gerrit for any change where `Autosubmit+1` is still set and status is MERGED (bot submitted before crash) to skip re-driving it, and either persist `re_drive_count` across restarts (e.g., in a lightweight state file or Gerrit change hashtag) or document that restart resets the counter intentionally with the tradeoff acknowledged.
findings.finding: The in-memory `wipChain` has no defined crash-recovery or restart-idempotency path: on process restart the record is lost, `re_drive_count` resets to 0 (potentially allowing more than N=5 total re-drives across restarts), and if the restart occurred mid-`submitting` phase the bot cannot determine whether the submit already succeeded — risking either a redundant submit attempt or indefinite re-driving of an already-landed change.
findings.location: AC — `wipChain` schema; AC — TOCTOU guard + bounded N=5 re-drive
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'On startup the loop reconciles in-flight state by querying Gerrit for any change with `Autosubmit+1` still set; if status=MERGED it skips to idle; `re_drive_count` is written to a local JSON state file on each increment and read back on startup so the N=5 bound survives restarts.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit unit-test assertions for the N=5-exhaustion hand-back side effects: verify that `Autosubmit` label is removed from every chain member AND a Gerrit comment naming `needs_rebase` is posted when `re_drive_count` reaches 5, not just that the counter reaches the limit.
findings.finding: The AC7 test list enumerates 'TOCTOU guard + bounded N=5 re-drive' but does not explicitly call out testing the hand-back observable behavior — label removal from every chain member and the Gerrit comment posting — that constitutes the user-visible outcome when N=5 is exhausted. This is the primary signal a remote `land` command reads to know its stack was handed back, making it a critical failure-path behavior for the user-facing flow.
findings.location: AC7 (Verification/tests) — 'the TOCTOU guard + bounded N=5 re-drive (SHA mismatch / main advancing mid-flight)'
findings.suggested_fix: In AC7's test list, append: 'hand-back side-effects at N=5: assert REST calls to remove `Autosubmit` label on all members and post the `needs_rebase` comment fire exactly once per exhaustion event, and that `wipChain` transitions to `idle`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a startup-reconciliation step to the AC: on boot, query Gerrit for the current wipChain candidate's status (MERGED/ABANDONED/NEW) to detect a mid-submit crash, and either persist `re_drive_count` to a lightweight durable store (file, Gerrit change hashtag, or SSM parameter) or explicitly document and accept the restart-resets-counter tradeoff with the N=5 bound treated as per-run not per-change.
findings.finding: The N=5 TOCTOU re-drive bound is held only in the in-memory `re_drive_count` field of `wipChain`. On any process restart (OOM kill, deploy, crash) the count resets to 0, silently defeating the safety bound: across k restarts, the lander can execute up to 5k re-drives. Additionally, if the process restarts while `phase=submitting`, the bot cannot distinguish 'submit succeeded before crash' from 'submit never fired', risking either skipping a landed change or re-attempting an already-merged one. The plan provides no startup-reconciliation or persistence mechanism for this state.
findings.location: AC — 'wipChain schema': 're_drive_count tracks the bounded TOCTOU re-drive loop (reset on selecting a new front)'; AC — TOCTOU guard: 'looping at most N=5 times'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief 'Alternatives considered' note (inline or as an ADR reference) documenting why Gerrit's native `autosubmit` plugin was not adopted — specifically whether it enforces fresh `Verified` on the post-rebase patchset SHA (the R5 load-bearing property), and whether its ACL model is compatible with the Contributors-votable `Autosubmit` label.
findings.finding: The plan builds a bespoke Autosubmit label + serial lander bot without evaluating Gerrit's first-party 'autosubmit' plugin (gerrit.googlesource.com/plugins/autosubmit), which implements the same opt-in-label → rebase → await-CI → submit pattern. The S1 ticket (843b-801f-9ef9-442d) acknowledges the naming collision ('must NOT be Gerrit's first-party autosubmit submit-plugin') but treats it as a naming concern, not a prior-art evaluation. The plan's 'Why' section references Zuul and Chromium CQ (via ADR-0040) but has no section evaluating the native plugin or explaining why a bespoke implementation is preferred over it.
findings.location: Why section and Dependencies section — no evaluation of Gerrit's native autosubmit plugin

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define an environment isolation strategy (e.g. a staging Gerrit project/branch, a feature flag, or a dry-run mode) so the autolander loop can be validated without touching production `Autosubmit`-labeled changes.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no dev/staging environment for the autolander service — there is no mention of running a non-production instance against a Gerrit sandbox or test namespace. All references to the loop target the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance directly (including the E2E AC). This means any code change to the loop is tested against production, and a buggy deploy risks real-stack mis-submits or label removals on live changes.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Verification/tests (E2E check) / Preconditions

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify an observability plan for the autolander daemon: structured log shipping target (e.g. CloudWatch Logs group), at least one metric (e.g. loop iteration count, submit success/failure), and a stale-heartbeat alarm so on-call is paged when the bot stops making progress.
findings.finding: The plan's observability coverage is limited to an application-level liveness heartbeat (S5 AC5). No infra-level logging sink, metrics emission, or alerting is specified for the daemon itself — there is no mention of structured log shipping, a CloudWatch/Datadog/Prometheus target, or an alarm that fires when the heartbeat goes stale or the loop errors repeatedly.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `wipChain` schema / S5 AC5 reference

findings.finding: No proving command or test strategy is described for any of the seven acceptance criteria; an executor has no way to verify the loop is correct without designing the test harness from scratch.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all seven ACs)

findings.finding: The plan introduces substantial new conditional logic (phase state machine with 5 states, re-drive loop up to N=5, FFO TOCTOU guard, FIFO ordering, merge-vs-non-merge rebase-selection branching) but contains zero testing specification — no unit tests, integration tests, or live/end-to-end acceptance criteria are mentioned, and no child ticket or deferral rationale is cited.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria and Scope sections

findings.finding: No proving command exists for any acceptance criterion — the plan contains zero test strategy, CI check, or verification command. An agent completing this work has no deterministic way to produce evidence that each AC is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all bullets)

findings.finding: The plan introduces a complex state machine (5 phases, FIFO selection, merge-vs-chain routing, N=5 re-drive loop, TOCTOU guard, fresh-Verified detection) but contains zero mention of a testing approach — no unit tests, integration tests, or acceptance test strategy for any of the specified behaviors, including multiple explicit failure paths (N=5 exhaustion → park, TOCTOU guard → re-drive, merge change never using /rebase:chain).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all items; Scope section

findings.finding: No proving command exists for any of the seven acceptance criteria — there is no test script, integration harness, or CLI invocation described that would produce concrete evidence of correctness for any claim.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all seven ACs

findings.finding: The plan specifies no testing approach for any of the new logic: the wipChain state machine, FIFO selection, rebase-routing branching (merge vs chain vs single), fresh-Verified SHA detection, TOCTOU guard, or the N=5 re-drive failure path that parks the change and surfaces `needs_rebase`. The N=5 exhaustion behavior is a new user-facing failure flow with zero test coverage mentioned.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all ACs; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'Unit tests cover rebase-routing (merge/chain/single), fresh-SHA Verified detection (carried-vote rejection), and wipChain phase transitions. An integration test drives the N=5 re-drive exhaustion path end-to-end against a Gerrit test double and asserts the change is parked with `needs_rebase`.'

findings.finding: The plan contains zero proving commands or described tests for any of its seven acceptance criteria — no executor can verify done-ness without inventing the entire test strategy.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all bullets)

findings.finding: No acceptance criterion has an associated proving command or verification step — the plan defines six behavioral ACs but provides zero concrete commands (unit test invocation, integration test, API query, log assertion) that an executor or reviewer can run to confirm each criterion is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all six ACs)

findings.finding: No testing plan exists for the substantial new state-machine logic introduced (wipChain phases, rebase-selection branching, fresh-Verified detection, TOCTOU guard, bounded re-drive loop). The plan contains zero mention of tests, test strategy, or test ACs, leaving the entire new behavior unvalidated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all ACs) and Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Testing' section to the plan with: (1) unit tests for the three-way rebase-selection rule using mocked RelatedChanges responses; (2) unit tests for fresh-Verified detection (carried-vote vs. new-patchset-vote); (3) state-machine tests cycling through all phase transitions; (4) a failure-path test for re-drive bound exhaustion (expect idle/error phase); (5) a TOCTOU test where main advances between submit-check and submit, verifying re-drive is triggered rather than stale submit.

findings.finding: The plan introduces substantial new branching logic (rebase-selection rule, Verified-freshness check, TOCTOU guard, FIFO ordering) but contains zero testing plan — no unit tests, integration tests, or deferred test child ticket — leaving all failure paths (SHA mismatch, stale vote, main advancing mid-flight, chain vs. single-change detection) completely unspecified for coverage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all ACs; Scope section

findings.finding: 'Park the change and surface needs_rebase' is not a specific observable outcome — 'park' and 'surface' are effort-framed verbs with no defined system-state change, making the criterion non-measurable in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — FFO TOCTOU guard AC (N=5 exhaustion clause)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a task to create a new ADR (e.g., ADR-0042) capturing the autolander's architecture: single-instance serialization, `wipChain` state machine and schema, 15 s poll cadence rationale, FIFO ordering contract, and TOCTOU re-drive design.
findings.finding: The autolander service introduces a novel architectural pattern (single-instance serialized state machine with `wipChain`, fixed poll cadence, FIFO contract, TOCTOU re-drive loop) but no new ADR or design-doc update is proposed to capture this decision.
findings.location: ## What / ## Acceptance Criteria / wipChain schema AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: '[ ] Create ADR-0042 (`infra/autolander/ADR-0042-autolander-core-loop.md`) capturing: single-instance serialization rationale, `wipChain` schema as the canonical definition, 15 s poll cadence justification, FIFO ordering contract, and TOCTOU bounded re-drive design. Reference from CLAUDE.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or explicitly reference the named S5 ACs) that specifies: (a) the bot starts in `paused` mode on first deploy and is manually un-paused after a smoke test; (b) rollback = `pause` the bot (via the emergency-stop mechanism) + redeploy previous image, documented and tested; (c) the `paused` → `selecting` transition is operator-gated, not automatic on startup.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new long-running bot that begins auto-submitting changes to `main` on deploy, but specifies no staged rollout strategy (flag, canary, or traffic-gating) and no explicit rollback procedure within this story. The deploy is deferred to S5 with no binding AC in this story that ensures S5 will cover staged rollout and rollback of the auto-submit behavior.
findings.location: Scope section; What section; no Rollout AC present
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'On first deploy, the loop starts in `phase=paused`; an operator must explicitly un-pause via the S5 admin interface after verifying the heartbeat and a dry-run rebase against a test change. Rollback procedure: set `phase=paused` via admin interface (immediate, no data cleanup required), then redeploy the prior image.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concurrency model for `infra/autolander/loop.py` (e.g., 'single asyncio event loop — all wipChain mutations occur in one coroutine; heartbeat is an awaited coroutine, not a thread') so that the serialization guarantee is mechanically grounded, not just asserted.
findings.finding: The concurrency model protecting `wipChain` mutations is unspecified: the plan says 'single instance' but does not say whether the loop is single-threaded, async (single-coroutine), or uses background threads (e.g., for the S5 heartbeat writer). If any concurrent access path exists, unsynchronized reads of `tested_shas` or `phase` could observe a partially-updated record.
findings.location: AC — `wipChain` schema; What — 'Holds a single in-flight `wipChain`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the What section: 'The loop runs as a single asyncio coroutine; all `wipChain` mutations and the heartbeat write occur on the same event loop, so no locking is required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove `paused` from the S2 `wipChain` phase enum and add a note that S5 will extend the schema with an emergency-stop mechanism; define `paused` only when S5 specifies its exact semantics.
findings.finding: The `wipChain` schema includes a `paused` phase that is explicitly and exclusively scoped to 'S5's emergency-stop' — a different, not-yet-planned story. This is a YAGNI abstraction: the `paused` state has no done-definition in S2's own ACs and serves only a hypothetical future requirement.
findings.location: AC5 wipChain schema: 'phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle, paused} (`paused` for S5's emergency-stop)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an IaC definition (Terraform module, CDK construct, Helm chart, etc.) for the autolander daemon's compute resource and SSM parameters, stored under `infra/`, with remote state + locking configured and a documented `plan`-before-`apply` workflow.
findings.finding: The plan specifies the Python module location (`infra/autolander/loop.py`) and references SSM-backed AWS secrets, but never describes how the daemon is provisioned or deployed — no IaC tool (Terraform, CDK, Ansible, Helm, etc.), no compute platform (ECS task, EC2 instance, K8s pod, Lambda), no remote state or locking, and no plan-before-apply discipline. Without codified deployment, the service is not reproducible, has no documented teardown path, and any manual provisioning creates untracked drift.
findings.location: Scope section / `infra/autolander/loop.py` file path

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the transport URL/posture for the autolander's pygerrit2 connection to Gerrit (e.g., 'always connects via the public HTTPS endpoint https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/a/ — never the loopback HTTP port — so the HTTP password is protected by TLS in transit'); add this as a security invariant alongside the SSM-backed credential note.
findings.finding: The plan does not declare the transport URL the pygerrit2 client uses to reach Gerrit — specifically whether it connects via the public HTTPS endpoint (TLS-terminated by nginx) or the loopback HTTP endpoint (http://127.0.0.1:8080). If the bot runs co-resident on the Gerrit host and uses the loopback URL, the SSM-backed HTTP Basic credential is transmitted in plaintext over loopback. The access posture (HTTPS vs. loopback HTTP) of the outbound privileged REST connection is undeclared.
findings.location: What section — 'Gerrit REST access uses the shared pygerrit2 client … the bot authenticates as the RebarBotNava service user via its SSM-backed HTTP password'
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's 'What' or 'Preconditions' section, add: 'The pygerrit2 client MUST be configured with the canonical HTTPS base URL (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/a/) — NOT the loopback HTTP endpoint — so the HTTP Basic credential is always protected by TLS. GERRIT_URL is a required config key (sourced from env/config, not hardcoded); the default must be the HTTPS URL. This is a deploy-time invariant checked in the E2E smoke test.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
